skill.name: ui-ux-pro-max
skill.description: UI/UX design guidance skill with local search over bundled design, accessibility, typography, color, chart, and frontend stack references.
skill.summary: Generate UI/UX design systems and implementation guidance from bundled frontend design references.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many critical and high matches, but semantic review found most are CSV documentation examples, markdown code fences, docs URLs, or words such as keystroke. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious intent was found. The remaining risk is medium because the skill ships executable Python helpers and tells users to run package-manager and local script commands.

medium_findings.title: Shell and Package Manager Commands in Instructions
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run shell commands for Python setup and local helper execution. The commands appear related to installation and use, but sudo, brew, winget, and script execution should be reviewed before running.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact commands are visible in the skill instructions. They are operational setup commands, not hidden execution, so the concern is user-consent and privilege risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Executable Helpers Read Local Skill Data
medium_findings.description: The bundled Python helpers load CSV files from the skill data directory and print search or design-system results. This is expected for the skill, but it is still executable code that reads local files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file reads are direct and confirmed, but they are constrained to bundled CSV data under the skill directory. I did not find network exfiltration or arbitrary path input tied to these reads.

low_findings.title: Static Network Findings Are Documentation URLs and Examples
low_findings.description: Many network findings are hardcoded documentation URLs or frontend examples in CSV rows. They do not appear to be runtime network calls made by the skill scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are reference material and public documentation or font URLs. The Python scripts inspected do not call requests, urllib, sockets, or a fetch API.

low_findings.title: Credential and Environment Findings Are Negative Examples
low_findings.description: The env and secret matches are mostly guidance examples that warn against exposing secrets, not code that reads or transmits real environment variables.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples discuss NEXT_PUBLIC, DATABASE_URL, API_SECRET, and runtimeConfig as educational guidance. No runtime credential access was found in the Python scripts.

low_findings.title: Critical Keyword Findings Are UI Terminology False Positives
low_findings.description: The keylogger and C2 keyword matches appear to come from benign UI terms such as keystroke, command palette, and control guidance in design reference CSV files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are UI performance or component guidelines. I did not find code that records keystrokes, creates command-and-control behavior, or hides malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Privileged Package Installation Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The instructions include a sudo apt command for installing Python on Ubuntu or Debian. This should require explicit user approval before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The sudo command is explicit and confirmed. It is a standard install command, so the risk is privilege escalation if run without informed consent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Script Execution Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The instructions repeatedly ask the assistant to run the bundled search.py helper. This is expected functionality, but users should understand that a local Python script will execute.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The execution pattern is directly documented and tied to the skill purpose. Review found local CSV search behavior rather than arbitrary command execution.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose time choosing UI patterns, colors, typography, and stack-specific details. This skill provides searchable design guidance and implementation checklists for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Searches bundled CSV references for UI styles, colors, typography, charts, landing pages, and UX rules.
Generates design-system recommendations from product type, industry, and style keywords.
Provides stack guidance for React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, SwiftUI, React Native, Flutter, Tailwind, and shadcn/ui.
Returns practical checklists for accessibility, responsive layout, animation, icons, and interaction quality.
Formats design-system output as terminal text or Markdown through the bundled Python helper.
limitations: It depends on bundled reference data, so it does not fetch the latest framework documentation.
Running the helper requires Python on the local machine.
Setup commands using package managers should be reviewed before execution.
It does not automatically test rendered UI or verify accessibility with browser tooling.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Product Interface
use_cases.description: Generate a design direction with recommended layout pattern, color palette, typography, effects, and avoid-list before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Guide Frontend Implementation
use_cases.description: Get stack-specific rules for React, Next.js, Vue, mobile frameworks, Tailwind, and shadcn/ui while building screens.

use_cases.title: Review UI Quality Before Delivery
use_cases.description: Check an interface for accessibility, spacing, responsive behavior, icon usage, hover states, and common visual polish issues.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Design Direction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use UI/UX Pro Max to recommend a design direction for a SaaS dashboard. Include layout pattern, style, colors, typography, and key accessibility checks.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Landing Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this landing page concept with UI/UX Pro Max. Suggest stronger sections, CTA placement, visual hierarchy, color choices, and interaction details.

prompt_templates.title: Build with a Specific Stack
prompt_templates.prompt: Use UI/UX Pro Max for a Next.js and Tailwind implementation. Provide stack-specific layout, image, font, accessibility, and performance guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Production Interface
prompt_templates.prompt: Use UI/UX Pro Max to perform a pre-delivery UX audit. Check accessibility, responsiveness, motion, loading states, forms, charts, and visual consistency.

output_examples.input: Design direction for a healthcare appointment dashboard.
output_examples.output: A calm clinical interface with clear navigation, high-contrast cards, restrained motion, accessible status colors, and readable sans-serif typography.

output_examples.input: Review a mobile e-commerce product page.
output: Increase tap targets to at least 44 pixels.
Move primary purchase action into a persistent bottom area.
Use product imagery with reserved dimensions to avoid layout shift.

output_examples.input: Create guidance for a shadcn/ui admin panel.
output_examples.output: Use semantic components, consistent variants, accessible forms, clear table structure, stable side navigation, and a compact dashboard layout.

best_practices: Start with a clear product type, industry, audience, and implementation stack.
Use the design-system output first, then request focused searches for typography, charts, accessibility, or stack rules.
Validate recommendations with real viewport checks, contrast testing, and user workflow review.
anti_patterns: Do not run setup or package-manager commands without reviewing them first.
Do not treat the bundled CSV guidance as a replacement for current official framework documentation.
Do not ship UI only from style recommendations without testing accessibility and responsive behavior.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps plan, build, and review product interfaces using bundled UI, UX, design-system, and frontend stack guidance.

faq.question: Does it generate final application code?
faq.answer: It can guide implementation, but the final code still depends on the assistant, project context, and requested framework.

faq.question: Does it need internet access?
faq.answer: The core search helper uses local CSV data. Some setup commands and documentation links may use the network if a user runs them.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it replace accessibility testing?
faq.answer: No. It provides checklists and guidance, but rendered interfaces still need contrast, keyboard, screen reader, and viewport testing.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill contains executable Python helpers and shell setup instructions. Review found no confirmed malicious intent.
